A-colis-installation-scenarios-tacas-202.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed TACAS 2020 paper (Springer LNCS, artifact-evaluated), DOI 10.1007/978-3-030-45237-7_14, by the tool authors; primary-authoritative. Grade confirmed by neutral+adversarial audit pass.
A-colis-installation-scenarios-tacas-202.relevance-description: Closest published prior art to static effect-analysis of shell for ops-orchestration: a command-generic symbolic-execution engine + per-command filesystem-effect specs applied to ~28k real Debian maintainer scripts; verbatim source of the engine/oracle-split-scales and translation-trusted-via-review+dash-comparison claims.

A-verified-interpreter-shell-vstte-2017.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed VSTTE 2017 working-conference paper (Springer LNCS), HAL author manuscript; primary. Audit raised the handoff's grading- and relevance-certainty from -0:SUSPECT to +1:SURE (neutral+adversarial passes agree).
A-verified-interpreter-shell-vstte-2017.relevance-description: Defines the CoLiS IR + a Why3-verified (sound AND complete) interpreter, deliberately using automatic SMT provers rather than Coq/Isabelle; grounds the 'no Coq' verdict and the parser/translation-not-formally-verified claims. NB: it verifies the interpreter (equivalence), not a symbolic over-approximation.

A-morbig-sle-2018.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed SLE 2018 full paper (ACM SIGPLAN), DOI 10.1145/3276604.3276615, by the tool authors; primary. Confirmed via neutral audit read (factual atoms already deterministically resolved in seq-predetermine).
A-morbig-sle-2018.relevance-description: Foundational primary work on statically parsing POSIX shell (Menhir incremental purely-functional LR + speculative/reentrant parsing) — the front-end any static effect-analysis of shell must solve first; verbatim source of the 'informal spec -> cannot prove parser correct / no absence-of-bugs claim' and the Dash/Bash conformance check.

B-colis-specification-of-unix-utilities-.grading-reasoning: Self-labeled non-refereed Technical Report (ANR, Feb 2019), HAL hal-02321691; authoritative+primary content (FOL/feature-tree specs) but no peer-review venue -> B. Confirms the handoff A->B rekey. Verified via neutral audit read.
B-colis-specification-of-unix-utilities-.relevance-description: Formal per-command catalogue of UNIX-utility filesystem effects as feature-tree (first-order-logic) constraints — directly the effect-spec layer a static effect-analyzer of shell needs; the 'UNIX-utils ontology seed' / canonical-fact basis referenced in plans/055 and plans/102.

A-lucassen-gifford-effect-systems-popl-1.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed POPL 1988 paper (15th ACM SIGPLAN-SIGACT POPL, pp.47-57), DOI 10.1145/73560.73564, Lucassen & Gifford (MIT LCS); seminal type-and-effect-systems work, primary. Neutral+adversarial audit passes both grade A/+1:SURE.
A-lucassen-gifford-effect-systems-popl-1.relevance-description: Foundational origin of polymorphic type-and-effect systems and static side-effect inference (READ/WRITE/ALLOC effects over regions, effect lattice combined by MAXEFF = least-upper-bound/union, latent effects on subroutines, effect masking). Verbatim source of the 'latent effect' lineage (plans/099) and the 'L-G effect-combination is commutative' characterization (plans/090). Mapping to shell/ops is conceptual/by-analogy, not a ready-made method.

A-horwitz-reps-binkley-sdg-slicing-topla.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed ACM TOPLAS 12(1):26-60, 1990, DOI 10.1145/77606.77608, Horwitz/Reps/Binkley (Univ. Wisconsin); the canonical system-dependence-graph interprocedural-slicing paper, primary-authoritative. Claim-support confirmed via audit grep (slicing-as-reachability verbatim).
A-horwitz-reps-binkley-sdg-slicing-topla.relevance-description: Source of the SDG/PDG 'retained dependence structure -> queries become graph reachability/slicing' thesis in plans/055 (verbatim: 'interprocedural slices ... each [pass] cast as a reachability'). Grounds Dorc's build-the-dependence-graph-once, answer-many-analyses-as-reachability design. Generic PL slicing, not shell-specific -> relevance by-analogy.

A-reps-horwitz-sagiv-ifds-popl-1995.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed POPL 1995 paper (22nd ACM SIGPLAN-SIGACT POPL, pp.49-61), DOI 10.1145/199448.199462, Reps/Horwitz/Sagiv (Univ. Wisconsin); the foundational IFDS paper, primary-authoritative. Headline atoms deterministically resolved in seq-predetermine (verbatim 'interprocedurally realizable paths' + 'the Tabulation Algorithm').
A-reps-horwitz-sagiv-ifds-popl-1995.relevance-description: The IFDS framework Dorc's fact-layer is designed onto (plans/055,099): finite distributive dataflow as realizable-path graph-reachability + tabulation summaries; the decidable-floor engine and the keep-state-facts-finite + transfer-distributive discipline. Directly load-bearing prior art.

A-reps-demand-interprocedural-cc-1994.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed CC 1994 paper (5th Intl. Conf. on Compiler Construction, Springer LNCS 786, pp.389-403), DOI 10.1007/3-540-57877-3_26, Thomas Reps; primary. Title/abstract confirmed via audit read.
A-reps-demand-interprocedural-cc-1994.relevance-description: The demand-driven interprocedural-analysis lineage cited (as 'Reps cc94') in plans/055 for Dorc's demand-query 'does host h need probing for fact F? compute only the queried slice'. Establishes computing summary info for a single program element on demand rather than exhaustively. Relevance by-analogy (demand-querying), not a direct method.

A-salcianu-rinard-purity-vmcai-2005.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed VMCAI 2005 paper (Springer LNCS 3385), Salcianu & Rinard (MIT CSAIL); primary by the analysis authors. A over B for refereed venue + primacy; -0:SUSPECT because VMCAI is mid-tier vs POPL/PLDI (a strict grader might say high-B). URL is PROPOSED (Springer DOI) pending curl-verify at migration. Both factual atoms deterministically resolved in audit precheck.
A-salcianu-rinard-purity-vmcai-2005.relevance-description: The mutation-domain template for static effect-analysis of shell/ops: compositional per-method purity/side-effect (MOD) summaries; verbatim source of 'a method that mutates only new objects is pure' (plans/055:22) and 'regular expressions that characterize the externally visible heap locations a method mutates' = the 'regex of touched locations' effect-characterization (plans/055:35).

A-smoosh-popl-2020.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed POPL 2020 paper (PACMPL vol.4, Greenberg & Blatt), DOI 10.1145/3371111; top-tier, primary, validated against 7 shells + 3 test suites. Confirmed via audit read (executable-POSIX-semantics core RESOLVED-DET).
A-smoosh-popl-2020.relevance-description: Executable mechanized small-step POSIX-shell semantics (Smoosh) — the soundness ceiling/reference any static effect-analysis of shell must respect; the CoLiS/smoosh 'how much propagation can be sound' base in plans/102:68. NB: the semantics is 1034 SLOC of Lem (compiles to OCaml), so plans' 'Smoosh (OCaml)' is ecosystem-accurate but Lem-cored.

A-jensen-moller-tajs-type-analysis-javas.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed SAS 2009 paper (Springer LNCS 5673), the seminal TAJS paper, Jensen/Moller/Thiemann; primary, heavily cited. A on impact/primacy; -0:SUSPECT because SAS is a notch below PLDI/OOPSLA and this initial paper reports preliminary precision (concurs with handoff). Claim-support confirmed via arbiter-read of the eval section.
A-jensen-moller-tajs-type-analysis-javas.relevance-description: Closest methodological precedent for sound abstract-interpretation of a messy dynamic language; the recency/strong-weak-update keystone for the effect/fact domain. Verbatim source of 'recency disabled -> <2% vs 87% before' (plans/055:33) and 'OOM with a 512MB limit' (plans/076:17), both confirmed in-paper (Section 5, richards.js/cryptobench.js).

A-distefano-scaling-static-analyses-face.grading-reasoning: CACM Aug 2019 contributed article (peer-reviewed/editorially-vetted), DOI 10.1145/3338112, by the Infer/Zoncolan leads (Distefano, Fahndrich, Logozzo, O'Hearn); primary first-hand industrial report. Industry-experience genre but venue+authority = A. Confirmed via arbiter-read: every attributed number verbatim-grounded.
A-distefano-scaling-static-analyses-face.relevance-description: The 'compositionality = scale, deploy at diff-time' template for static effect-analysis of ops at scale. Verbatim source of the scale figures (100M Hack LOC in <30 min on a 24-core server; ~1 trillion LOC/day) and the deployment lesson (plans/076:18, 055:68): 'the fix rate ... was near zero ... the fix rate rocketed to over 70%' when moved from batch/offline to diff-time code review.

A-arzt-bodden-reviser-incremental-ifds-i.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed ICSE 2014 paper (flagship SE venue), DOI 10.1145/2568225.2568243, Arzt & Bodden (TU Darmstadt/EC SPRIDE); primary tool paper (Reviser on Heros/Soot) with empirical eval. An engineering extension to IFDS/IDE rather than new theory, but venue+primacy = A. Confirmed via arbiter-read: the attributed ~80% figure verbatim-grounded.
A-arzt-bodden-reviser-incremental-ifds-i.relevance-description: The incremental-recompute prior art for a static effect-analysis fact-layer: Reviser's 'clear-and-propagate' updates interprocedural IFDS/IDE results in response to code change, producing results identical to a full recomputation. Verbatim source of the '~80% saved, identical results' figure (plans/055:66, 076:65): 'Reviser produces the same results as a full recomputation, can save up to 80%' / 'saves, on average, about 80% of analysis time'.

B-dozer-icse-seip-2022.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed ICSE-SEIP 2022 (SE-in-Practice track), DOI 10.1145/3510457.3513060, Horton & Parnin (NCSU); B not A because the SEIP track + the text self-declares a 2-page short/tool paper with a thin 62-command eval (extended-abstract), a step below the full archival A papers. Not lower than B: genuine peer review, NSF-funded. Confirms the handoff A->B rekey. Syscall-altitude framing confirmed via arbiter-read.
B-dozer-icse-seip-2022.relevance-description: On-domain shell-to-Ansible prior art: the syscall-altitude insight Dorc's effect-observation rests on -- 'shell commands and Ansible modules can only change the system state by communicating with the kernel via the system call (syscall) interface' (verbatim) -- grounding plans/076:77's 'Dozer-style oracle/effect derivation' and naming task composition as the open hard problem.

A-cousot-abstract-interpretation-popl-19.grading-reasoning: Cousot & Cousot, 'Abstract Interpretation: A Unified Lattice Model...', 4th POPL (Jan 1977); the founding abstract-interpretation paper, primary-authoritative and among the most-cited works in PL -- grade A on provenance. NB read-depth = provenance-only: the local scan is IMAGE-ONLY (empty OCR layer), so claim-support was NOT adjudicated (status GAP); the over-approximation theory is independently covered via the SPA textbook + the co-cited TAJS.
A-cousot-abstract-interpretation-popl-19.relevance-description: Abstract-interpretation foundation: the sound-by-construction over-approximation discipline ('everything unmodeled collapses to top') that plans/055:25 rests the MayMutate lattice on. Consumed indirectly via the Moller-Schwartzbach SPA textbook; the plan co-cites TAJS for the same guarantee.

A-biabduction-popl-2009.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed POPL 2009 (ACM SIGPLAN-SIGACT), DOI 10.1145/1480881.1480917, Calcagno/Distefano/O'Hearn/Yang; the foundational bi-abduction / compositional-shape-analysis paper (the inference basis of Infer), primary-authoritative -> A. Confirmed via arbiter-read: bi-abduction jointly inferring frame + anti-frame is verbatim ('infers anti-frames (missing portions of state) and ... frames'; the bi-abduction entailment A * ?anti-frame |- G * ?frame); 'footprint' present (:1441,:1505).
A-biabduction-popl-2009.relevance-description: The infer-side of Dorc's frame/footprint knob (kBURDEN): compositional per-procedure pre/post (anti-frame + frame) inference, independent of callers; maps to inferring an oracle's effect-frame vs declaring it (plans/090 D2/F2). Concept-level relevance (separation-logic shape analysis is not directly liftable; the footprint/frame-inference idea is).

A-mokhov-build-systems-a-la-carte-icfp-2.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed ICFP 2018 (PACMPL vol.2), DOI 10.1145/3236774, Mokhov/Mitchell/Peyton Jones; top-tier archival PL venue, executable Haskell models, primary -> A. Confirmed via precheck-RESOLVED-DET + arbiter-read: every attributed term verbatim (Minimality Def 2.1; Scheduler x Rebuilder factoring :476; topological/restarting/suspending schedulers :481-489; dirty-bit/verifying-trace/constructive-trace/deep-constructive rebuilders :492-514; early cutoff :153-158; self-tracking :12).
A-mokhov-build-systems-a-la-carte-icfp-2.relevance-description: The exact vocabulary for Dorc's skip-thesis (plans/076 4b): Scheduler x Rebuilder = apply-DAG-walk x skip-decision; constructive-traces (store value, keyed by input content-hash, share across users -> cloud-build) ground the cross-host probe-verdict memoization lever; early-cutoff = idempotent-rewrite-yields-identical-state-fact -> dependents still skip.

B-moller-schwartzbach-static-program-ana.grading-reasoning: Authoritative Aarhus lecture-notes textbook (Moller & Schwartzbach, continuously updated 2008-2025; this copy dated 2025-04-29), CC-BY-NC-ND; secondary pedagogical synthesis, no peer-review venue -> B (confirms the handoff A->B rekey). Confirmed via TOC read: the plan's section-number cites are exact -- 2.5 Control Flow Graphs, ch.4 Lattice Theory, ch.5 Dataflow Analysis with Monotone Frameworks (5.8 Forward/Backward/May/Must), ch.8 Interprocedural Analysis, ch.9 Distributive Analysis Frameworks (9.4 IFDS, 9.6 IDE).
B-moller-schwartzbach-static-program-ana.relevance-description: The methodological scaffolding under Dorc's analyzer (plans/021,055): CFG construction, lattices/monotone-framework dataflow, forward-may orientation, interprocedural analysis, IFDS/IDE distributive frameworks -- cited section-precisely. Generic (not shell-specific); low adjudication power (textbook 'supports' is fuzzy). NB the 021 'slicing (SPA 8-9)' co-mention is imprecise -- SPA has no slicing chapter; the plan's real slicing source is Horwitz-Reps-Binkley (055:32).

A-scholz-souffle-datalog-cc-2016.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed CC 2016 (ACM, Oracle Labs authors Scholz/Jordan/Subotic/Westmann), DOI 10.1145/2892208.2892226; the definitive Souffle compile-Datalog-to-native paper, primary -> A. Confirmed via arbiter-read of the eval: the attributed 'OpenJDK points-to in 35 s' is verbatim (Table 4 'Total 35 [s]' :1152; prose 'within 35s on a commodity desktop system' :1179 for the context-insensitive points-to on OpenJDK7); semi-naive evaluation + compile-to-relational-algebra (RAM) + 'billions of tuples' (:131) all verbatim; '34x faster than the best state-of-the-art solver' :1042.
A-scholz-souffle-datalog-cc-2016.relevance-description: The engine that scales a Datalog fact-base to native speed (plans/055 'Datalog-compiled-to-native ... OpenJDK points-to in 35 s'; 076 Souffle materialized-fact RAM = the memory wall). NB 'Souffle why-trees / provenance' (plans/055:49) is a real Souffle-TOOL feature but from a LATER Souffle paper, absent from this CC2016 paper; 055:49 prose-attributes it to the tool (no CC2016 bracket-key) -> not a CC2016 misattribution.

A-spall-mitchell-rattle-perfect-dependen.grading-reasoning: Peer-reviewed OOPSLA 2020 (PACMPL vol.4, Art.169), DOI 10.1145/3428237, Spall/Mitchell/Tobin-Hochstadt; primary system paper (rattle) with formalized hazards + impl/eval -> A. Confirmed via arbiter-read: the plan's 'Rattle borrows the CPU pipeline hazard taxonomy (RAW/WAR/WAW + speculative write-before-read)' is verbatim-grounded in 6.4 :689 -- 'CPUs use hazards ... read/write, write/write and write/read hazards [Patterson & Hennessy] -- our terminology is inspired by their approaches ... Rattle also stalls the pipeline (stops speculating) if it detects potential hazards' -- plus the 2.6/4.1 read-write & write-write hazard definitions and the speculate-then-restart discipline (2.8 :176 'aborting the build and restarting without any speculation').
A-spall-mitchell-rattle-perfect-dependen.relevance-description: Closest build-systems prior art for Dorc's hoist/elide-as-speculation (plans/090 D7): a forward build over a straight-line script with no declared deps, recovering 'perfect dependencies' by tracing file accesses and skipping unchanged-hash commands; its speculate-detect-hazard-fall-back-to-non-speculative-re-run discipline is the directly transferable execution backstop. (The hazard-FORMALIZATION sequel 'Forward Build Systems, Formally', CPP 2022, is a separate, not-acquired work the plan cites distinctly.)
